compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart
compiled_state.description:
## Why

Completion-verification is single-pass and, by contract, emits findings ONLY for FAILING
criteria (`reconcile_verdict` flips a PASS-with-findings to FAIL). On a PASS it persists just
signed counts via the attestation — no per-criterion record exists anywhere, and the FAIL-only
sidecar never fires. So a successfully-closed ticket has no durable per-criterion completion
audit, unlike its plan and code reviews.

## What

1. Extend the verifier's output contract with a positive per-criterion `criteria[]` array — one
   `{criterion, met, citation, kind}` entry per acceptance/success criterion evaluated (met
   status, a source-code or attestation citation, and its codebase-verifiable / operator-attested
   kind). This rides ALONGSIDE the failures-only `findings`; a PASS keeps `findings == []`.
2. Emit a completion sidecar on PASS (not only FAIL) persisting that array plus the verdict,
   runner, model, and material fingerprint.

Completion stays single-pass — this adds a per-criterion result list, NOT a four-pass structure.

## Schema-version strategy (explicit)

Do NOT bump the existing FAIL schema. Keep `SCHEMA_FAIL = "completion_verifier_fail_v1"`
unchanged (so `latest_fail_verdict` and the existing FAIL-record tests are untouched). Add a
SECOND constant `SCHEMA_PASS = "completion_verifier_pass_v1"` for PASS records; `build_payload`
picks the schema by verdict. This keeps `test_fail_close_persists_durable_verdict` and
`test_pass_close_leaves_no_fail_record` green (a PASS record is a different schema, so
`latest_fail_verdict` still returns None on a PASS).

The `kind` field vocabulary is the CLOSED set `{"codebase-verifiable", "operator-attested"}`
(matching ADR 0043's two criterion kinds); `met` is a bool; `citation` reuses the shared
`Citation` shape (nullable).

## Scope (enumerated edit targets)

- `src/rebar/llm/contracts.py` — **load-bearing**: `CompletionVerdict` is a pydantic `BaseModel`
  with no `extra="allow"`, so pydantic v2 DROPS any `criteria[]` the agent emits unless it is a
  declared field. Add a `Criterion` sub-model (`{criterion: str, met: bool, citation: Citation |
  None, kind: str}`) and a `criteria: list[Criterion] = Field(default_factory=list)` field to
  `CompletionVerdict`.
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_verdict.schema.json` — add the OPTIONAL `criteria[]` property
  (items `{criterion, met, citation, kind}`); `findings` stays required, `criteria` optional. The
  model↔schema PARITY test
  (`tests/interfaces/store/test_completion_verifier.py::test_completion_verdict_pins_to_schema`)
  asserts the model's field set equals the schema's property set, so it MUST be UPDATED in lockstep
  to include `criteria` (it will NOT pass unchanged) — this is an expected, in-scope test edit.
- `src/rebar/schemas/completion_reconcile_input.schema.json` — **required because this schema is
  `additionalProperties: false`**: add a `raw_criteria` (array) property, else the workflow's
  `completion_reconcile` step would reject/not-carry the criteria. (The verify step's `criteria`
  output must be threaded into the reconcile step — see the YAML edit below.)
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gates/completion-verification.yaml` — in the `completion_reconcile`
  step's `with:`, thread `raw_criteria: ${{ steps.verify.outputs.criteria }}` (alongside the
  existing `raw_verdict`/`raw_findings`), so the agent's positive array reaches the reconcile op.
- `src/rebar/llm/workflow/gate_ops.py` — `completion_reconcile(ctx)` reads `raw_criteria` from
  `ctx.inputs` and sets `result["criteria"] = list(ctx.inputs.get("raw_criteria") or [])` on the
  reconciled verdict BEFORE `validate_structured`, so the workflow (close-gate) path carries
  `criteria[]` end-to-end — not only the bespoke `verify_completion` path.
- `src/rebar/llm/reviewers/completion_verifier.md` — update the prompt: instruct the agent to
  emit `criteria[]` with one met-record per evaluated criterion (each with citation + kind).
  `findings` stays failures-only.
- `src/rebar/llm/completion.py` — `reconcile_verdict` must NOT flip a PASS that carries a
  populated `criteria[]` but empty `findings` (it keys the flip on `findings`; ensure `criteria[]`
  is not stripped and add the regression test).
- `src/rebar/llm/completion_sidecar.py` — the existing constant is named `SCHEMA` (value
  `"completion_verifier_fail_v1"`). Do NOT rename it (that would churn the FAIL reader + its
  tests); keep `SCHEMA` as the FAIL schema and ADD a second constant
  `SCHEMA_PASS = "completion_verifier_pass_v1"`. `build_payload` branches on the verdict
  (PASS → `SCHEMA_PASS` + `criteria[]`; FAIL → unchanged `SCHEMA` + `findings`); add
  `latest_pass_record(ticket_id)` mirroring `latest_fail_verdict` but schema-guarded to
  `SCHEMA_PASS`. `latest_fail_verdict` (guarded to `SCHEMA`) is untouched, so a PASS record is
  invisible to it and the existing FAIL tests stay green.
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` — on the PASS path (after the verdict is confirmed
  PASS, before `_verdict_manifest` signs), add a best-effort `completion_sidecar.emit(result,
  material=…, repo_root=repo_root)` (swallow its own errors — persistence is observability, never
  load-bearing on the close). Set `result.setdefault("ticket_id", resolved_id)` first.

## Compatibility & rollback

`criteria[]` is OPTIONAL — an older agent/response that omits it yields today's behavior (PASS
counts only), and the failures-only `findings` invariant is unchanged, so nothing regresses. No
backfill. Reverting stops the PASS-path emit and drops the field; existing FAIL records stay
readable (their schema is untouched).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A completion PASS returns a `criteria[]` array with one met-record per evaluated criterion
      and `findings` stays empty. Oracle: a unit test on `reconcile_verdict` feeding a PASS
      verdict with a populated `criteria[]`, asserting it is NOT flipped to FAIL and the array
      survives; plus a test that the `CompletionVerdict` pydantic model round-trips `criteria[]`
      (proving it is not dropped).
- [ ] A completion PASS emits a `completion_verifier_pass_v1` sidecar persisting the per-criterion
      array. Oracle: an integration test that closes a ticket through the gate with a stub verifier
      returning the new PASS shape and asserts on the emitted event JSON (via the real sidecar
      emit path), plus that `latest_pass_record` reads it back.
- [ ] Live end-to-end: running the real completion verifier (`[agents]` extra + API key) on a
      fixture ticket whose criteria are met produces a PASS with a populated `criteria[]`
      persisted to the sidecar. Oracle: a live/E2E test exercising the real agent path, gated on
      LLM availability (skipped with a recorded reason otherwise).
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` pass.
compiled_state.title: completion-verification lossless PASS capture (per-criterion record)
